topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet solitude of my evening. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison, my notoriously stern history teacher. My stomach clenched, a tight, unpleasant knot forming just beneath my ribs. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening until my teeth ached. He had somehow found my profile. A slow, burning resentment began to pool in my chest, thick and suffocating. I felt a surge of pure irritation, a desperate need to see him stumble, to watch his carefully constructed composure crack. I quickly minimized the app, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain a semblance of calm, though the heat rising in my cheeks betrayed the turmoil inside.

[story 2]

I found myself checking my phone obsessively, the glow illuminating my face in the dim light of my room. There, a post from Mr. Harrison—a picture from a school event, captioned with a pithy, overly academic observation. A cold, sharp feeling washed over me, instantly souring my mood. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing loudly in the silence. Every line of his previous lectures, every critique he’d ever leveled at my essays, suddenly seemed magnified, twisted into something petty and malicious. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space, my movements jerky and agitated. A bitter taste coated my tongue; I wanted to lash out, to make him regret ever thinking he could observe me.

[story 3]

It was the sheer audacity of it that truly unsettled me. Seeing Mr. Harrison’s curated online presence, the casual way he shared snippets of his life, felt like a deliberate slight. I scrolled through his recent activities, and a wave of profound displeasure washed over me, heavy and inescapable. I gripped my pen so hard my knuckles turned white. The air in the room seemed to thicken, pressing down on my chest. I didn't want to confront him; instead, a simmering fury built up, a low, vibrating tension that made my skin crawl. I just wanted him to understand the sheer wrongness of his intrusion, to feel the weight of my silent disapproval radiating off me like heat.
